output:
SBC Validator — guided walkthrough
  config vendor: audiocodes    ruleset: 2026-06-07

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  STAGE 1 — INGEST  (raw vendor config → one normalized model)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  SBC FQDN: sbc-teams-01.contoso.com
  Teams leg:
      transport=tls   mTLS=yes   SRTP=yes   keep-alive=yes
      codecs=['PCMU', 'G722']   trusted roots in store: 7
  Carrier leg: transport=tls   keep-alive=yes   codecs=['PCMU', 'G722']
  Media realm 'Default': advertised=80.0.0.40   symmetric-RTP=yes

  → Every check below reads THIS model, never the raw vendor syntax.

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  STAGE 2 — VALIDATE  (each domain reasons over the model)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  [A] syntax & semantic baseline (well-formed, no dangling refs)
        ✓ clean (or silent: nothing in the source to judge)
  [B] interop: TLS transport, OPTIONS keep-alive, header normalization
        ✓ clean (or silent: nothing in the source to judge)
  [C] the 2026 wedge: mTLS, the 7 required roots, SRTP, cert + chain
        ✓ clean (or silent: nothing in the source to judge)
  [D] NAT / media: advertised address, symmetric RTP (one-way audio)
        ✓ clean (or silent: nothing in the source to judge)
  [E] codec: cross-leg overlap, transcode, DTMF
        [INFO    ] E.CODEC.AMRWB_NOT_OFFERED  —  Teams leg does not offer AMR-WB (2026 prioritized wideband)
                  why: Since 2026 Microsoft offers AMR-WB on Direct Routing trunks and prioritizes it when the SBC offers it back. Without it, calls negotiate another common codec (no outage), at reduced audio quality. Also confirm the SBC tolerates the unknown payload type 121 in Teams' SDP offer.
                  fix: Add AMR-WB to the Teams-leg codec list if the SBC supports it (HD audio); otherwise verify graceful fallback against the 2026 Teams SDP offer.
  [G] routing & classification (fires only if the source carries it)
        ✓ clean (or silent: nothing in the source to judge)
  [S] security: ACL default-deny, broad CIDR, IPv6, shadowing
        ✓ clean (or silent: nothing in the source to judge)

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  STAGE 3 — VERDICT  (severity-weighted; any CRITICAL forces BLOCK)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Risk score: 0/100      VERDICT: PASS
  Findings: INFO 1

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  STAGE 4 — PREDICT  (model the call: TLS → SIP → SDP → media)
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  Outcome: STABLE
  Predicted: call connects with two-way audio using PCMU.

  SBC     Microsoft Teams SIP proxy
          -------------------------
            --> TLS ClientHello
            <-- TLS handshake OK
            --> OPTIONS (keep-alive)
            <-- 200 OK
            --> INVITE  SDP offer: PCMU,G722
            <-- 100 Trying
            <-- 180 Ringing
            <-- 200 OK   SDP answer: PCMU
            --> ACK
           ===  RTP PCMU bidirectional  ===

────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  STAGE 5 — NEXT
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
  PASS — ready for the 2026 CA migration. Re-run after any config change.
